questions.question: When is the ‘International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances’ observed?
questions.explanation: ‘International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances’ is marked every year on August 30, across the world. The day is observed to honour and pay tribute to people who have faced enforced disappearances. On December 21, 2010, the United Nations General Assembly officially proclaimed August 30 as the International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances. The Assembly also welcomed the adoption of the International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance.

questions.question: Which country has passed the world’s first law to force tech companies to offer alternative payment systems?
questions.explanation: South Korean lawmakers passed the world’s first law to force tech giants such as Google and Apple to offer alternative payment systems on their app stores. The country is set to amend the ‘Telecommunications Business Act’, the nation’s primary telecommunications law. The legislation would allow developers to bypass the tech giants’ proprietary payment systems and could help shape how the firms collect fees and exert power over developers and users.

questions.question: Which Indian city has prepared a Draft Regional Plan-2041 (DRP-2041)?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is preparing the ambitious Draft Regional Plan-2041 (DRP-2041), which aims to evolve it into a “future ready” and economically prosperous area with citizen-centric infrastructure. The NCRPB covers entire Delhi, eight districts of Uttar Pradesh, 14 districts of Haryana and two districts of Rajasthan. The regional plan says that population in National Capital Region (NCR) is expected to grow to around 7 crore by 2031 and to about 11 crore by 2041 The draft plan envisages on making multilevel parking complexes, peripheral parking, Superfast trains and heli-taxi services, Trauma centres on highways and Helipads for high-rise buildings.

questions.question: Jezero Crater is a part of which planet?
questions.explanation: NASA’s Mars rover have successfully collected its first rock sample for return to Earth. The rock sample, which is about the thickness of a pencil, could have slipped deeper into the tube during a series of planned vibrations. Perseverance arrived in February at Mars’ Jezero Crater, in search of rocks to find evidence of ancient life. The crater is believed to be the home of a lakebed and river delta, billions of years ago. NASA plans to launch more spacecraft to retrieve the samples collected by Perseverance.

questions.question: Which e-Commerce platform has announced the launch of ‘Kisan’ store?
questions.explanation: Amazon India has launched the Kisan (farmer) store a store on its platform, meant exclusively for farmers. The Amazon Kisan store was launched by Agriculture Minister, Narendra Singh Tomar. Farmers can now buy farming tools, seeds, crop protection and other farming tools on the Amazon platform. This aims to make agri-inputs available to buyers at competitive prices with the added convenience of delivery at their doorstep.

questions.question: Which institution released ‘Atlas of Mortality and Economic Losses from Weather, Climate and Water Extremes’?
questions.explanation: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) released the Atlas of Mortality and Economic Losses from Weather, Climate and Water Extremes (1970–2019). As per the report, every third death (35 per cent) from extreme weather, climate or water stress in half a century was in Africa. The African continent accounts for only 17 per cent of global population. Four droughts (1973 and 1983 in Ethiopia, 1981 in Mozambique and 1983 in Sudan) were responsible for 89 per cent of the total deaths in Africa from weather, climate and water extremes in the last 50 years. Almost 99 per cent of all reported deaths occurred in low-to-lower-middle-income countries in Africa.

questions.question: Which Indian state has launched the e-ILP platform for Inner Line Permit (ILP) system in the state?
questions.explanation: Manipur Chief Minister N Biren Singh virtually launched the e-ILP platform for effective regulation of the Inner Line Permit (ILP) system in the state. The e-ILP platform aims to regulate the entry, exit and tracking of visitors who are overstaying beyond the permitted duration. The ILP system came into effect in Manipur on January 1, 2020. ILP is a document that Indian citizens from other states are required to possess in order to enter states like Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, Nagaland and Manipur. It aims to protect the local populations from large-scale migration.

questions.question: Manish Narwal, who was in the news recently, is associated with which sport event?
questions.explanation: Indian Shooter Manish Narwal has bagged a Gold Medal at the Tokyo Paralympics in the P4 Mixed 50m Pistol SH1 event. The 19-year-old shooting champion shot a total of 218.2 which is a Paralympic record. Another Indian shooter Singhraj Adana has bagged the Silver in the P4 Mixed 50m Pistol SH1 event and bronze in the P1 men’s 10m air pistol SH1.

questions.question: Which city is the venue of the 5th World Conference of Speakers of Parliament?
questions.explanation: 5th World Conference of Speakers of Parliament is being held in Vienna on Sept 7th and 8th. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla reached Vienna to attend the 5th World Conference of Speakers of Parliament by the Austrian Parliament, Inter-Parliamentary Union and the United Nations. The two-day conference will begin on Sept 7. The World Conference of Speakers of Parliament is held regularly every five years with the goal to reinforce the Parliamentary dimension of global governance.

questions.question: Which city is the venue of the IUCN World Conservation Congress?
questions.explanation: Some 28% of the 1,38,374 species assessed by the International Union for the Conservation of Nature (IUCN) for its survival watch list are now at high risk of vanishing forever. The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2020 is being held in Marseille, France from September 3 to 11, 2021. Habitat loss, over-exploitation and illegal trade have hammered global wildlife populations for decades, and climate change is now kicking in as a direct threat as well as reported by the IUCN. The event was originally scheduled in June, 2020 but was rescheduled due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
